Analysis

The Verdicts

01

The Toss

Winning The Toss Means Almost Nothing

Toss winners win 50.6% of matches — barely better than a coin flip. But the decision you make with it is everything.

Bat after winning: 44.4% Field after winning: 53.8%

That's a 9.4% swing from one call.

02

Phase Analysis

Death Overs Decide Matches. Not Powerplays.

Everyone fixates on powerplay starts. The data disagrees.

PP gap: +0.99 RPO Death gap: +1.94 RPO

Winners outscore losers by 1.94 runs per over in the death — nearly 2× the powerplay gap.

03

Hidden Pattern

Over 7 Is The Free Wicket Nobody Takes

The moment the powerplay ends, scoring collapses.

Over 6: 8.73 RPO Over 7: 7.02 RPO ← dead zone Over 1: 6.35 RPO

Over 7 is cheaper than the opening over.

04

Chase Difficulty

The Wall Is At 175, Not 200

The real cliff comes earlier than people think.

Chasing 150s: ~72% Chasing 160s: ~50% — coin flip Chasing 170s: ~40%

Every run above 170 is worth more than one below it.

05

Momentum

Momentum Is Real. Captains Ignore It.

After a 12+ run over, the next over averages 9.5 runs. After a quiet (<6) over: 7.8 runs.

Big→Big: 31% Quiet→Big: 19%

That's a 12% momentum edge the batting side carries.

06

Era Shift

Pre-2022 IPL Is A Different Sport

The scoring acceleration since 2022 has been historic.

2008: 8.31 RPO · 11 sixes/match 2026: 9.86 RPO · 19 sixes/match

Sixes nearly doubled. Pre-2022 batting records are a different conversation.

07

CSK Anomaly

CSK Win 59% Of Tosses. Always.

Win toss + choose bat: 59%  win.
Win toss + choose field: 59%  win.

Exact same number. It's not the coin — it's the culture.

What-If Analysis

The Butterfly Effect

Using 1,239 matches of historical win probability data, we can ask: if one thing had been different, what changes?

IPL 2019 Final · May 12, 2019 · Hyderabad

MI vs CSK — Won By 1 Run.
The Closest Possible Margin.

MI Actual
149
Historical win rate: 27%
If MI scored +10 in death
159
Win rate: 27% → 37%
Watson dismissed for 30
~108
CSK win chance collapses to 94%

Archetype layer: Watson is pre-2021 era, but the CSK 2019 XI had just 1 Death Finisher-class batter among those traceable in the 2021–25 data. Anchor-class batters cannot sustain 14+ RPO in death overs — that's why the chase stalled at 148. One player's archetype decided a final.

SRH · All Seasons · Toss Decision Analysis

SRH Left ~5 Matches
On The Table.

Won toss, chose BAT
39
Win rate: 35.9%
Won toss, chose FIELD
60
Win rate: 48.3%
Matches left on table
~5
Correctable strategic bias.
Not bad luck.

The verdict: 36% when batting vs 48% when fielding — a 12-point gap from one decision. Every playoff race has been this close.

Every Team · Every Season · Over 7

The Over 7 Trap:
A Free Wicket Nobody Claimed.

Over 6 (last PP over)
8.73
RPO · field restrictions on
Over 7 (dead zone)
7.02
RPO · cheaper than Over 1
Scoring drop
1.71
RPO fall-off from over 6→7

If every team bowled their best spinner in over 7 instead of 5: they'd save ~0.71 runs on average and face a batting team still resetting after powerplay restrictions lifted. The over exists. Nobody uses it.

Kohli's 2016 Season · Era-Adjusted

What Would Kohli's Record Season
Look Like In 2026?

2016 actual
973
runs · RPO era: 8.41
Era-adjusted to 2026
~850
equivalent difficulty-adjusted runs
Raw projection
~1,135
if today's balls/match rate applied

The record stands. But context shifts everything. At 8.31 RPO (2008) vs 9.86 RPO (2026), facing the same deliveries produces ~19% more runs by default. Cross-era comparisons need an asterisk.

Win Probability Model

Death Score → Win Probability

Batting first. Historical outcomes. The breakeven is ~55 runs in the death.

Death ~30 runs
23%
already fighting uphill
Death ~40 runs
36%
still below 50/50
Death ~50 runs
41%
approaching the wall
Death ~60 runs
48%
flips to slight favourite
Death ~70 runs
67%
clear favourite territory
Death ~80 runs
75%
dominant. 7 in 10 wins.

UMAP + KMeans · IPL 2021–25

Every Player Has A Type

12 batting dimensions · 11 bowling dimensions · reduced to 2D · 4 clusters each.

Hover a card to see what defines each archetype.

⚡ BATTERS (85 players)

🏔️
Anchor
High average. Low dot-ball rate. Plays for the long innings, not the six. Gets out bowled or lbw — they play straight.
Dhawan · Dube · Markram
29 of 85 batters
💥
Aggressor
Highest boundary%. Goes hard in all phases. Consistency is not the priority — damage is.
Russell · Karthik · David
13 of 85 batters
☠️
Death Finisher
Peaks in overs 16–20. SR jumps 30+ points in the death phase. Built for the final five overs.
Rahane · Jacks · Kock
37 of 85 batters
⚡
Powerplay Enforcer
Highest SR in overs 1–6. The one you need at the top when restrictions are on. Fades in the middle.
Suryavanshi · Arya · Head
6 of 85 batters

🎯 BOWLERS (149 players)

🗡️
Death Specialist
Lowest economy in overs 16–20. The one you call at 18.3 with 12 to defend. Rarest, most valuable.
Narine · Ali · Chawla
40 of 149 bowlers
⚡
Powerplay Enforcer
Best economy in overs 1–6. Takes wickets when the field is up. Sets the tone before the middle overs.
Marsh · Parag · Sams
49 of 149 bowlers
🌀
Wicket-taking Spinner
High stumped%. Beats the batter in the air and off the pitch. Middle-overs pressure specialist.
Sharma · Jacks · Markram
14 of 149 bowlers
🛡️
Containment Bowler
Lowest economy across all phases. Won't take many wickets. Will keep the scoring rate honest every over.
Sakariya · Parnell · Zampa
46 of 149 bowlers

Structural Break · Chow Test

IPL Has Two Timelines

The scoring shift post-2022 isn't a vibe — it's a statistically confirmed break in the time series.

2008 – 2021 Era
8.31
RPO average
Trend: +0.043 RPO/yr — gradual climb
11 sixes/match (2008) → slowly rising
2022 – 2026 Era
9.86
RPO average
Trend: +0.327 RPO/yr — nearly 8× faster
19 sixes/match — almost double 2008
⚗ Chow F = 9.65 · p = 0.0020 · break CONFIRMED at 2022

Common Wisdom vs Data

Myths The Numbers Killed

Hover each card to see what the data actually says.

Myth 01
"The powerplay decides the match."
↙ hover to see the data
Reality
Death overs have 2× the impact. Winners outscore losers by +1.94 RPO in the death vs +0.99 in the powerplay. Wrong phase, wrong focus.
Myth 02
"200 is the unassailable target."
↙ hover to see the data
Reality
The real cliff is at ~175. Chasing 160s is a 50/50. The non-linear wall appears well before 200 — confirmed by Breusch-Pagan heteroscedasticity (p=0.000).
Myth 03
"Winning the toss is everything."
↙ hover to see the data
Reality
Toss winners win 50.6% of matches. The toss itself is neutral. What destroys teams is making the wrong decision with it.
Myth 04
"Momentum doesn't exist in cricket."
↙ hover to see the data
Reality
AC₁ = +0.023, p < 0.0001. Over-to-over autocorrelation is statistically significant across 47,599 within-match observations. It's not vibes.

Random Facts

Did You Know?

50.5%
Toss win rate. Barely better than the coin itself.
1,218 matches · all seasons

5 Statistical Tests · Plain English

The Numbers Behind The Numbers

📍
Structural Break
p = 0.0020
2022 is a statistically confirmed inflection point. The scoring game genuinely changed — not just a trend, a break.
📈
Autocorrelation (DW)
0.959
Durbin-Watson = 0.96. Seasons aren't independent — a high-scoring year predicts the next. The trend has momentum.
🔁
Momentum AC
p < 0.0001
Within-match over-to-over autocorrelation confirmed. A big over genuinely makes the next over more likely to be big.
📊
Phase Variance (Levene)
p < 0.0001
Death overs aren't just higher-scoring — they have unequal variance. More chaotic, more decisive. That's why they matter more.
🌊
Heteroscedasticity (BP)
p = 0.0001
Win probability isn't linearly distributed across target scores. The "wall at 175" is non-linear — a logit model fits better than OLS.

22 Grounds · All Seasons

Every Ground Has A Personality

Avg 1st-innings total tells you whether a ground is a batting paradise or a bowler's fortress. Bat-first win% below 50% means the ground rewards chasing.

Powerplay Wickets · All Seasons

The Skeleton Key: Powerplay Wickets

Lose 0 in the powerplay and you win 59% of the time. Lose 4+ and it drops to 16%. That's a 43-point swing decided in the first 6 overs.

59%
batting-first win rate · n=269

48%

40%

25%
batting-first win rate · n=138
4+ wkts in powerplay
16%
batting-first win rate · n=51

Data-Driven · IPL 2026

Our Prediction

based on current 2026 form + playoff venue fit + 8 signals including H2H vs strong opponents. not financial advice.

▸ how it works
SIGNALWTSOURCE 2026 season win rate30%standings_2026.json · 2026-05-27 EWMA win rate10%2022–25 · decay=0.50/season (2025 counts 8× 2022) death batting RPO10%60% 2026 + 40% hist blend · overs 16–20 death bowling RPO8%60% 2026 + 40% hist blend · runs conceded playoff venue fit9%Ahmedabad (Final 70%) + Mullanpur (Elim/Q2 30%) archetype ratios9%UMAP + KMeans · 2021–25 H2H vs strong opponents6%win rate vs last-4-yrs playoff teams · 2022–26 win dominance5%avg margin of victory in 2026 (runs or wicket-equivalent) home advantage5%home vs away win-rate delta 2026 NRR4%tiebreaker · (runs/overs scored − conceded) toss-decision edge4%field% − bat% win rate
elimination: max_pts < 16 → score zeroed · backtest 16 seasons walk-forward · top-4: 75% vs 40% random

⚠ Ball-by-ball CSV current to 2026-05-24 — standings updated to 2026-05-27 via standings_2026.json. Eliminated (max pts < 4th-place current 16): CSK, DC, KKR, LSG, MI, PBKS
